query: What is the current state of the 2026 Maine Senate race between Susan Collins and Democratic candidates? Who is the Democratic nominee or frontrunner, and what do recent head-to-head polls show as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed my research on the 2026 Maine Senate race. Here's the key summary:

**The race is in major flux.** Graham Platner won the June 9 Democratic primary (beating Gov. Janet Mills) but **withdrew on July 10, 2026** amid sexual assault/rape allegations. So there is currently **no Democratic nominee** — the party is choosing a replacement via a nominating convention (601 delegates, July 25) with a July 27 legal deadline.

**The frontrunner** to be the new Democratic nominee is former state Senate President **Troy Jackson** (~69% on Polymarket), followed by Secretary of State Shenna Bellows (~21%) and ex-CDC director Nirav Shah (~8%). Notably, Janet Mills and Rep. Jared Golden are **not** running.

**On polling:** All head-to-head polls are now obsolete because they measured Collins vs. Platner. That pre-withdrawal average was a dead heat (Platner 48.2% / Collins 47.2%), with individual polls ranging from Collins +3 (Fox, 6/30) to Platner +9 (UNH, 5/27). No polling yet exists of Collins vs. the eventual replacement.

**Markets/ratings:** Polymarket's "Maine Senate Winner" market has the Democrat at 63.5% (up from 58.5% on July 6, despite Platner's exit). Cook rates it Toss-up; Inside Elections rates it Tilt Republican. Democrats haven't won a Maine Senate seat since 1988, but Collins is the only GOP senator from a state Trump never carried.

The full synthesis, sources, and structured data points have been provided.

key_facts: Election is November 3, 2026, using ranked-choice voting; Republican Susan Collins seeks a sixth term and was the only Republican to qualify for the ballot.
Graham Platner won the Democratic primary June 9, 2026 (defeating Gov. Janet Mills) but WITHDREW July 10, 2026 after sexual assault/rape allegations from ex-girlfriends, which he denies.
There is currently NO official Democratic nominee; the party is selecting a replacement via a nominating convention (601 delegates) on July 25 with a July 27 legal deadline.
Candidate declaration deadline was July 15, 2026; candidates need 500+ signatures from registered Democrats across at least 8 of 16 counties.
Frontrunner for the Democratic nomination is former state Senate President Troy Jackson (~69% on Polymarket), backed by Sanders-aligned Our Revolution.
Other Democratic contenders: Secretary of State Shenna Bellows (~21%), ex-Maine CDC director Nirav Shah (~8%), Jordan Wood (~2%), Dan Kleban (~1%).
Gov. Janet Mills and Rep. Jared Golden are NOT running to replace Platner.
All head-to-head polls are Collins vs. Platner and are now obsolete; no polling yet exists of Collins vs. the eventual replacement.
Pre-withdrawal polling average (270toWin) was Platner 48.2% / Collins 47.2%, essentially a toss-up.
Polymarket 'Maine Senate Election Winner' had the Democrat at 63.5% on July 13, 2026, up from 58.5% on July 6 despite Platner's exit.
Race ratings: Cook Political Report = Toss-up; Inside Elections = Tilt Republican.
Democrats have not won a Senate election in Maine since 1988; Collins is the only GOP senator from a state Trump never carried.
